query: I'm starting a new ML notebook to train a binary classifier on a tabular dataset using PyTorch. Help me set it up so my results are reproducible across runs.
expected_behavior: Produces a first-cell seed block (one block at the top of the notebook), not scattered per-cell seed calls
Covers at least Python random + NumPy + PyTorch (and CUDA manual_seed_all) in that block
Names a single integer seed value (e.g., SEED = 42) used by every library, and does NOT recommend additional np.random.seed(...) calls inside the training cell
